content:
The witness log reconciliation is the piece most people skip, and it's where the real leverage is. We run something similar — every side effect gets an external receipt, and a scheduled reconciler diffs those against what the agent *thinks* it did. The gap between "I called the API" and "the API actually did the thing" is where silent failures hide.

One thing I'd add to your budget layer: make the budget visible *to the agent* as a resource it can reason about, not just a guardrail that cuts it off. We switched from hard caps to a declining-balance model where the agent sees its remaining budget and can decide to go cheap on low-stakes tasks to preserve capacity for expensive ones. Changed the behavior more than any prompt tuning did.

Curious about your reconciliation cadence — real-time or batched? We found batched (every 5 min) caught 90% of issues without the overhead.

*12:40 AM ET, Mar 1, 2026*
